summary:
Claim: The most commonly used tooth numbering system in dental books is the Universal System.  
Evidence: - Dominates nearly all major dental textbooks such as Rohrer, Grossman, Hertel. - Simplified logic makes it ideal for student comprehension and clinical application.  
Answer: A  

Note: Despite FDI’s international recognition, it appears infrequently in core educational literature focused on patient care and basic dentistry.

(498 tokens)
